# ADR Index

16 decision(s): 16 architecture, 0 slice, 0 batch.

## Architecture

| id | title | status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0002 | ADR frontmatter schema | accepted |
| 0003 | Invariants are enforced by a hook, not by memory | amended |
| 0004 | Verification harness and in-repo standing invariants | amended |
| 0005 | Write routing and the bounds of assistant memory | accepted |
| 0006 | /init-method installs the kit, and refuses to install a hook that would brick the repo | amended |
| 0007 | Slash commands are vendored per repo, and a repo's edits to them survive | accepted |
| 0008 | Where a hook framework already owns the pre-commit slot, the doc checks join it rather than fight for the file | accepted |
| 0009 | A citation into another repo's corpus is qualified by repo name, and the linter skips it | accepted |
| 0010 | There is a fourth kind: the live doc, which describes how something behaves now | accepted |
| 0011 | Slices carry a Definition of Done in Given/When/Then | accepted |
| 0012 | Cite the governing ADR at the code site | accepted |
| 0013 | Surface the reuse decision; a generated capability index | accepted |
| 0014 | Rename the method to Stele | accepted |
| 0015 | Distribution by npx-delivered vendoring, not a runtime dependency | amended |
| 0016 | The global-conventions link is a personal convenience, not an install step | accepted |
